title: base_url validated at JmapChatClient::new() prevents misuse at API boundary
description: JmapChatClient::new() now validates base_url via url::Url::parse() and rejects paths, query strings, and fragments. A contributor might suggest deferring validation to the first network call to simplify construction, or loosening the validation to accept any URL. The validation at construction time is correct: (1) it follows Rust's 'parse early, validate early' principle — an invalid URL is a programming error, not a runtime condition; (2) path components in base_url would silently corrupt all request URLs (/.well-known/jmap, /api, etc.) since they're appended without a path join; (3) fragment and query rejections are correct per HTTP semantics — these have no meaning on an origin URL. Do not defer validation.
close_reason: Decision record: current implementation is correct; no code change needed
